title: IMD says monsoon to hit Andamans around 16 May; warns of severe heatwaves in northwest India - Mint

full_text:
IMD says monsoon to hit Andamans around 16 May; warns of severe heatwaves in northwest India IMD said conditions are turning favourable for the southwest monsoon to advance into the Andaman Sea, even as severe heatwave conditions intensify across parts of Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh and adjoining regions. NEW DELHI: Conditions are turning favourable for the crucial southwest monsoon to advance into parts of the south Bay of Bengal, Andaman Sea and the Andaman and Nicobar Islands around 16 May, even as large parts of northwest, west and central India brace for intense heatwave conditions in the coming week, according to the India Meteorological Department’s (IMD) latest update. A well-marked low-pressure area over the southwest and adjoining west-central Bay of Bengal is aiding the progress of the monsoon current, while multiple upper-air cyclonic circulations over north and central India and a western disturbance over Jammu and adjoining regions are shaping weather patterns across the country, the weather forecaster said on Wednesday. The southwest monsoon typically reaches the Andaman and Nicobar Islands around the third week of May before advancing towards the Kerala coast. In 2025, the monsoon reached the Andamans on 13 May. The Indian mainland usually sees the onset on 1 June, marking the beginning of the southwest monsoon season (June to September). The weather forecaster has projected below-normal rainfall this monsoon season amid emerging El Nino conditions. The monsoon is vital for Indian agriculture, helping irrigate farms and replenish reservoirs. The IMD said isolated heavy to very heavy rainfall is likely over northeast India during 14-19 May and over Tamil Nadu, Puducherry & Karaikal, Kerala & Mahe and south interior Karnataka during 14-17 May. Even as southern states are likely to see rain-led relief, it warned that heatwave to severe heatwave conditions are expected to persist across northwest India, west India and parts of central India during several days of the week. West Rajasthan is likely to witness heatwave conditions at many places, with severe heatwave conditions in isolated pockets during 14-15 May. West Madhya Pradesh may also experience severe heatwave conditions during 14-17 May. Heatwave conditions are also forecast in isolated pockets over Uttar Pradesh, Punjab, Haryana, Delhi, east Rajasthan, east Madhya Pradesh, Vidarbha, Chhattisgarh, Telangana and parts of Gujarat and Maharashtra during 14-19 May. Warm night conditions are likely over parts of Rajasthan, Vidarbha, Marathwada and Madhya Maharashtra, while Konkan and Goa may see hot and humid weather on 14 May. IMD said maximum temperatures are expected to rise gradually by 4-6 degrees Celsius over northwest India during 14-19 May, while central and east India could see temperatures increase by 2-4 degrees Celsius till 17 May. On 12 May, maximum temperatures crossed 42 degrees Celsius across most parts of Rajasthan, Gujarat, Vidarbha, Marathwada and west Madhya Pradesh. Barmer recorded the country’s highest maximum temperature at 48.3 degrees Celsius. Vijay C. title: Weather Today [13 May, 2026]: Check IMD Rain Predictions, Heat Alert, Snow & Thunderstorms Across Delhi NCR, Mumbai, Chennai, Karnataka, Rajasthan, Jammu, Hyderabad & More — Full City-Wise Forecast - The Sunday Guardian

full_text:
Weather Today, 13 May 2026: India is witnessing one of the sharpest weather contrasts of the season while scorching heat continues to dominate western and central states, thunderstorms, hailstorms and heavy rainfall are disrupting life across northern and eastern India. According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), temperatures have already crossed 45°C in parts of Rajasthan and Gujarat, while intense thunderstorms swept through Delhi-NCR, Punjab, Jammu and Kashmir and Uttar Pradesh.
The changing weather pattern has raised concerns over public health, crop damage, travel disruptions and rising electricity demand while at the same time, early monsoon signals are beginning to emerge over the southern Bay of Bengal and Andaman Sea.
Will it Rain or Snow in the Hills?
The Himalayan belt is expected to witness unstable weather conditions through the week as Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand and Jammu & Kashmir may receive thunderstorms, light rainfall and isolated hailstorm activity. Higher reaches of Kashmir could also witness fresh snowfall during late-night hours due to a western disturbance.
Srinagar is likely to remain cool compared to the plains with minimum temperature at 8°C and Maximum at 14°C while tourist movement in hilly areas may experience temporary disruptions because of slippery roads, reduced visibility and gusty winds.
Why Is The Heat Building Up So Fast Across India?
Meteorologists say the extreme temperatures are being driven by a strong anticyclone hovering over northwest India and this weather system traps hot air close to the surface, creating what experts call a “heat dome.”
Several factors are accelerating the heat:
- Clear skies allowing direct solar heating
- Dry northwesterly winds from Pakistan and Rajasthan
West Rajasthan recorded night temperatures over 5°C above normal, making conditions uncomfortable even after sunset.
Where are the Heat Wave Alerts?
IMD has advised people to avoid outdoor exposure between 12 PM and 4 PM.
Delhi-NCR Weather Today
Minimum: 33°C | Maximum: 44°C
Delhi witnessed dust storms, thunderstorms and rainfall on Tuesday night, offering temporary relief from severe heat however, temperatures are expected to remain dangerously high through the week. Wind speeds may touch 40-60 kmph during thunderstorm activity while air quality remains in the moderate category despite brief rainfall.
Chennai Weather Today
Minimum: 29°C | Maximum: 37°C
Chennai is likely to remain humid with chances of isolated thunderstorms during evening hours due to moisture incursion from the Bay of Benga while rsidents may experience sticky conditions through the day despite cloud cover.
Mumbai Weather Today
Minimum: 29°C | Maximum: 34°C
Mumbai is expected to remain relatively stable with humid coastal conditions and partly cloudy skies while light rain activity cannot be ruled out in suburban pockets.
Hyderabad Weather Today
Minimum: 26°C | Maximum: 39°C
Hyderabad continues to battle intense daytime heating with isolated thunderstorm activity expected by evening and the city may witness gusty winds alongside cloud formation later in the day.
Karnataka Weather Today
Minimum: 24°C | Maximum: 38°C
Several districts of Karnataka are likely to receive thunderstorms with lightning and gusty winds while Interior Karnataka continues to remain warmer than coastal regions.
Uttar Pradesh Weather Today
Minimum: 30°C | Maximum: 44°C
Lucknow and adjoining regions remain under severe heatwave conditions despite isolated thunderstorm activity while eastern UP may witness scattered rainfall later tonight.
Rajasthan Weather Today
Minimum: 30°C | Maximum: 43°C
Rajasthan remains among the hottest regions in the country with temperatures nearing 46°C in western districts. Dry winds and extremely low moisture levels continue to intensify heat stress.
Jammu and Kashmir Weather Today
Minimum: 8°C | Maximum: 14°C
Thunderstorms and hail activity are likely across parts of the valley and Jammu division while strong winds may affect road connectivity in higher areas.
Punjab Weather Today
Minimum: 27°C | Maximum: 41°C
Punjab witnessed thunderstorms and gusty winds on Tuesday and weather instability is expected to continue. Adampur recorded one of the lowest minimum temperatures in north India at 16.6°C earlier this week.
Ahmedabad Weather Today
Minimum: 30°C | Maximum: 44°C
Ahmedabad remains under heatwave warning as temperatures continue to hover above normal levels while night temperatures are also expected to stay unusually high.

How Will Rising Temperatures Impact?
The rapidly increasing temperatures are expected to affect several sectors simultaneously.
- Crop stress in wheat and vegetable-producing states
- Travel delays due to dust storms and reduced visibility
- Rising pollution levels due to stagnant air conditions
Hospitals across major cities are already reporting an increase in heat-related illness cases.
Delhi NCR Weekly Weather Forecast
Delhi’s Air Quality Today – May 13, 2026
Delhi’s Air Quality Index (AQI) remained in the “Moderate” category following Tuesday night’s dust storm and rainfall.
Fine dust particles continue to remain suspended due to strong winds and dry conditions in neighboring states while sensitive groups are still advised to limit prolonged outdoor exposure.
Major Cities Air Quality Across India
FAQ’s: All About Today’s Weather
1. Are There Any Travel Or Safety Warnings?
Yes. IMD has warned of thunderstorms, lightning, hailstorms and heatwave conditions across several states. Travelers are advised to monitor local weather updates.
2. Will Temperatures Rise Across The Country?
Most northern and western states are expected to remain hotter than normal over the next seven days.
3. Will There Be Any Rain Today?
Yes. Rainfall and thunderstorms are likely in Delhi-NCR, Jammu & Kashmir, Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Assam, Meghalaya, Karnataka and Tamil Nadu.
4. Where Will It Snow Today?
Higher reaches of Jammu & Kashmir and isolated Himalayan regions may receive light snowfall.
5. Is The Monsoon Arriving Early?
Conditions are becoming favorable for southwest monsoon advancement over the Andaman Sea later this week.
